title: lobelia erinus var. bellidifolia
intro: Lobelia erinus var. bellidifolia, also known as the blue lobelia, is a flowering plant species in the Lobelia genus, native to South Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a respiratory stimulant, helping to ease bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lobelia erinus var. bellidifolia are attributed to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve symptoms of coughs, colds, and other respiratory infections. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to help promote relaxation and alleviate respiratory distress.
